overview.description: AI-powered geospatial intelligence platform for agent-driven spatial analysis
overview.description_long: PangeAI builds autonomous AI agents that turn natural language queries into geospatial outputs — no GIS expertise required. The platform delivers instant spatial analyses and simulations across energy (renewable siting, corridor risk), natural capital (deforestation tracking, restoration mapping), and insurance (hazard scoring, portfolio exposure). Product is pre-launch ('coming soon') as of early 2026.

history.origin_story: Two Stanford graduates who spent 15+ years in geospatial science, environmental, and energy sectors built PangeAI to democratize access to geospatial data without requiring GIS expertise.

key_milestones.event: Selected as best tech to address Nature Finance at Lindau Nobel Laureate Meetings
Emerged from stealth with $1M seed investment (Dec 2025)
Featured in Forbes for physically-oriented agentic AI tools (Mar 2026)

awards: Best tech to address Nature Finance — Lindau Nobel Laureate Meetings (2025)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: PangeAI builds on top of AI agents and LLMs for geospatial use, so foundation model improvements could commoditize parts of their stack. However, the specialized domain integration of geospatial data pipelines, vertical-specific workflows (energy siting, nature-based solutions, insurance hazard scoring), and proprietary data sourcing create meaningful differentiation beyond a simple LLM wrapper.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Geospatial intelligence is a real and growing market driven by climate, energy transition, and insurance underwriting needs. However, the product is pre-launch ('coming soon') as of early 2026 with no announced customers, so PMF is unproven. Established GIS vendors (Esri, Google Earth Engine) and climate analytics firms represent competitive incumbents.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Only $1M raised at pre-seed stage with a product still in development. Very limited runway for a dual-geography team (Prague + Silicon Valley). No revenue announced.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Pre-launch product, minimal funding ($1M pre-seed), and unproven PMF make this high-risk. The domain specialization and Stanford-pedigree team are positive signals, but the company is still in early stages with no confirmed customers or revenue.

metadata.notes: Registered as PangeAI Europe s.r.o. in Karlín, Prague; US entity as PangeAI Inc. in Sunnyvale/San Francisco. Johanna von der Leyen is related to the EU political family (daughter of Ursula von der Leyen). Product was 'coming soon' as of March 2026. Emerged from stealth December 2025 with $1M investment from Miton (Czech VC). Founded by Stanford grads with 15+ years geospatial experience.
